name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-100
title: deployed settings.json missing hooks section — dispatchers not auto-firing
description: The deployed .claude/settings.json is missing the hooks section (PreToolUse, Stop, SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit registrations). The source .claude/features/rabbit-cage/settings.json also lacks hooks. This means the dispatchers are not auto-registered with Claude Code and won't fire on events. Hooks should be re-registered by running install.py / run_publish_loop after a fresh install, but the current workspace state has no hooks in the deployed file. Root cause: likely publish_settings ran in isolation (overwriting hooks added by publish_hook) without a subsequent full publish_hook run. This was a pre-existing state at Plan D wave start (M .claude/settings.json in gitStatus).

history.note: FALSE ALARM at branch level. The committed state on feature/meta-contract-api-libraries has all 4 hooks correctly registered (PreToolUse scope-guard, Stop, SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit). What Plan D's wave observed in ws50 was a local uncommitted change — Plan D's verification flow (likely install.py or run_publish_loop invocation during end-to-end testing) mutates the deployed settings.json in the working tree but does not commit the result. Fresh clones get the correct state. The interesting question 'why does Plan D's verification clobber settings.json without committing it?' is a separate concern best left for the rabbit-cage feature's audit wave to root-cause and harden (verification steps should not have observable side effects on the working tree, or should clean up after themselves). Not filing a new B/B for this since the audit wave is the natural place to surface it.
